title: BlackBerry Ltd: Business Model and Competitors 2026
text: BlackBerry Ltd is now fully remade as an enterprise software and services shop, with its energy centered on two core businesses: QNX embedded IoT software and Secure Communications cybersecurity. Under CEO John Giamatteo, BlackBerry sold off non-core businesses like Cylance, and in February 2025 the company sold its Cylance endpoint security business to Arctic Wolf, which boosted cash reserves and strategic clarity. The BlackBerry Software Support Life Cycle provides detailed timelines for the availability, support, and end-of-life milestones of BlackBerry enterprise products, helping customers plan upgrades and transitions. The company provides support globally with advice, analysis, implementation, and migration expertise, with Technical Support Services included in all enterprise software subscriptions. BlackBerry's transformation from smartphone manufacturer to enterprise software provider continues to demonstrate progress in building sustainable revenue streams through recurring software revenue models. The company leveraged its expertise in security, and funded the transformation by monetizing its extensive real estate holdings and library of intellectual property, while managing the ramp down of its hardware business.
